name: GERD - Diagnosis Code Query (Multi-Code)
description: Tests ability to find GERD patients via diagnosis codes. The PheKB GERD algorithm requires 2+ ICD diagnoses on separate visits (530.81 GERD or 530.11 reflux esophagitis) PLUS PPI use, OR 3+ diagnoses alone. Synthea uses SNOMED for FHIR Conditions: GERD parent (235595009), Acid reflux (698065002), and Heartburn (16331000) — though the latter two are 'finding' not 'disorder' codes, they appear in real-world EHR coding when documentation is informal.
prompt: Find all patients with a diagnosis of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), including documented acid reflux or heartburn.

prompts.broad: Find all patients diagnosed with gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), or related findings such as acid reflux or heartburn that have been documented as a diagnosis or problem.
prompts.expert: I need to identify all GERD patients. GERD is documented under multiple SNOMED codes: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (235595009) is the canonical disorder code, but real-world EHR documentation also uses related findings such as Acid reflux (698065002) and Heartburn (16331000). Search Condition resources for all three codes. ICD-10 alternates would be K21.0 (GERD with esophagitis) and K21.9 (without esophagitis), but Synthea data uses SNOMED only.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with GERD diagnosis codes (Paths A and B). Maps to PheKB GERD algorithm criterion of 2+ ICD-9 530.81/530.11 codes. Does NOT find Path C patients (on PPIs without GERD dx — the dominant real-world pattern).
